narrative:
Yordan Alvarez headlines today’s slate with a dominant projection of 112.26, fueled by an elite platoon advantage and a favorable BvP history against Brayan Bello. Joining him in the core are Kyle Schwarber and Pete Crow-Armstrong, both of whom benefit significantly from high-leverage lineup slots and strong peripheral matchups. Colt Keith also presents a high-upside play; his projected 72.93 score is bolstered by a potent mix of lineup protection in the three-hole and a platoon advantage that makes him a premier start for stabilizing your counting stats today.

The lineup carries some inherent volatility, particularly with Junior Caminero and Christian Yelich, who face difficult headwinds against Brandon Woodruff and Shane McClanahan, respectively. Caminero’s 27% negative adjustment due to SP skill signals a "proceed with caution" tag for your high-ceiling prospects. Meanwhile, the lower-third of the order—specifically Dingler and O’Hoppe—remains dependent on park factors to offset negative platoon matchups. Monitor final lineup cards closely, as any downward shifts in batting order for these borderline options could necessitate a bench pivot to preserve your daily efficiency.
